task_updates.executor_notes: Read-only audit completed. Grep `post_simple_topic|post_sectioned_topic` across `src/` and `tests/` found the expected topic-editor surface plus runtime/core test coverage. Code touch points verified in `src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py`: `WRITE_TOOL_NAMES` currently includes `post_simple_topic`, `post_sectioned_topic`, `watch_topic`, `update_topic_source_messages`, `discard_topic`, `record_observation`, `finalize_run` and does not include `post_topic`; `TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` still uses the role label `BNDC live-update topic editor`, advertises decision tools as `post_simple_topic, post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic, update_topic_source_messages, discard_topic, record_observation`, and contains the media-to-sectioned steering under `## Structured Document Topics (post_sectioned_topic with blocks)` saying multi-section topics and ANY image/video/embed/external media link should use `post_sectioned_topic` blocks; `TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS` defines `post_simple_topic` as text-only with media forbidden and `post_sectioned_topic` as the structured/media-capable tool with required `body` and `source_message_ids`; dispatch routes only `{post_simple_topic, post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic}` to `_dispatch_create_topic_tool`; normalized block writeback currently only writes `args["blocks"]` for `post_sectioned_topic`; `action_by_tool` lacks `post_topic`; legacy `post_simple_topic` media/raw-media-ref guard still rejects with `post_simple_cannot_attach_media_use_post_sectioned_topic`; legacy `post_simple_topic` source/author-count guard still rejects multi-source/multi-author simple posts; current `post_sectioned_topic` blocks-required guard accepts either non-empty legacy `sections` or normalized `blocks`; collision rejected-action map covers only simple/sectioned/watch; `_tool_summary` publish summary recognizes only simple/sectioned/watch; `_summary_for_tool` stores legacy `post_sectioned_topic` as `{body, sections, blocks?}` and simple/default as `{body, media}`; auto-shortlist `summary.suggested_actions` still tells the model `post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic/update sources, or discard_topic`; `build_rejected_transition` allows only `rejected_post_simple`, `rejected_post_sectioned`, and `rejected_watch`; idempotency/retry helpers also have write-tool sets that will need `post_topic` in later tasks. Publisher path verified: `_publish_topic` treats any topic whose summary has `blocks` as structured, hydrates source metadata, calls `render_topic_publish_units`, then builds send units. `render_topic_publish_units` normalizes via `normalize_topic_document`; for a one-block intro topic it emits exactly one text unit containing `## Live update: <headline>` plus the intro text and per-block sources, then emits one media/external unit per resolvable `media_ref`. The intro branch intentionally skips `**title**` section markup because title lines are only added when `block["type"] == "section" and block_title`; no `Share with your followers` string exists in this repo by grep, so no duplicate CTA path was found here. Tests enumerated: `tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py` contains direct runtime assertions, including the media-bearing simple rejection at lines around 305-337, tool-list equality/length around 301, action vocabulary audit around 1380-1437, legacy simple calls, and several `post_sectioned_topic` blocks/media validation/publishing tests around 2140 onward; `tests/test_topic_editor_core.py` contains `render_topic_publish_units`, `normalize_document_blocks`, media-ref normalization, and rejected-transition core tests; `tests/test_topic_editor_media_understanding.py` checks topic-editor tool schema registration for vision tools; `tests/test_backfill_live_update_topics.py`, `tests/test_live_update_editor_publishing.py`, and `tests/test_live_update_editor_lifecycle.py` have no direct grep hits for `post_simple_topic`, `post_sectioned_topic`, `render_topic_publish_units`, `normalize_document_blocks`, or `build_rejected_transition`; `tests/test_live_update_editor_*` remain relevant as related editor suites for later full verification. Grep found no tests asserting obsolete prompt strings like `use sectioned for ANY media` or `BNDC live-update topic editor`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added `post_topic` to `WRITE_TOOL_NAMES` and inserted the new `post_topic` tool immediately before `post_simple_topic` in `TOPIC_EDITOR_TOOLS`. The new tool schema requires exactly `["proposed_key", "headline", "source_message_ids", "blocks"]`, declares `blocks` with `minItems: 1`, reuses the existing block item shape (`type`, optional `title`, required `text`, block-level `source_message_ids`, and canonical/shorthand `media_refs`), includes optional `parent_topic_id`, `notes`, and `override_collisions`, and omits top-level `body` and `sections`. The `post_topic` description includes the required editorial-complexity sentence: one intro block is a single-beat story even with media, and section blocks are only for distinct contributors/angles/sub-stories. Prefixed both legacy descriptions with `[DEPRECATED — use post_topic]`; legacy input schemas were left unchanged. Removed legacy-description steering from `post_simple_topic` toward `post_sectioned_topic` and did not add any media-to-sectioned steering to `post_sectioned_topic`.

Updated only the auto-shortlist `summary.suggested_actions` model-visible string from `post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic/update sources, or discard_topic` to `post_topic, watch_topic/update sources, or discard_topic`. Verified no remaining `post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic` suggested-action string remains. Threshold logic and surrounding active-topic assembly were not changed.

Read-only publisher trace confirmed no publisher edit is needed in this batch. `_publish_topic` checks `summary.blocks`, normalizes the topic with `normalize_topic_document`, hydrates source metadata, then passes the topic to `render_topic_publish_units`. `normalize_document_blocks` returns existing `summary.blocks` as normalized block objects, preserving an intro block as `{type: "intro", title, text, source_message_ids, media_refs}`. In `render_topic_publish_units`, section title markup is emitted only for `block["type"] == "section" and block_title`; the intro branch intentionally passes without adding a `**title**` line. For an intro block, `block_content` becomes `header + "\n\n" + block_content`, a single text unit is appended, and then one media/external unit is appended per resolvable `media_ref` after the text. This yields exactly one text unit plus media units for a one-intro-block `post_topic` summary, with no orphan section header.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: SC2 satisfied: `post_topic` is before `post_simple_topic`, `WRITE_TOOL_NAMES` includes `post_topic`, schema has `blocks.minItems = 1`, top-level `body`/`sections` are absent from the new tool, required fields are exactly `["proposed_key", "headline", "source_message_ids", "blocks"]`, both legacy descriptions start `[DEPRECATED — use post_topic]`, and the legacy `post_sectioned_topic` description has no media-to-sectioned steering.

SC5 satisfied: auto-shortlist suggested actions now contain `post_topic, watch_topic/update sources, or discard_topic`; grep confirms the old `post_sectioned_topic, watch_topic` string is gone. No threshold or surrounding assembly logic changed.
SC7 satisfied: publisher trace confirms one intro block renders as one header/body text unit followed by one media/external unit per media_ref, and only section blocks can add `**title**` markup.

task_updates.executor_notes: Extended dispatch for `post_topic` without changing summary storage (T4 remains pending). `_dispatch_tool_call` now routes `post_topic` to `_dispatch_create_topic_tool`; normalized block writeback now writes `args["blocks"]` for both `post_sectioned_topic` and `post_topic`; `action_by_tool` maps `post_topic` to `post_topic`; the blocks-required guard is split so `post_topic` rejects unconditionally on `not has_blocks` with `rejected_post_topic` / `post_topic_requires_blocks`, while legacy `post_sectioned_topic` preserves existing `not has_sections and not has_blocks` semantics and reason; the collision rejection map includes `post_topic: rejected_post_topic`; `build_rejected_transition` now accepts `rejected_post_topic`; idempotent collision override retry and tool input hint helper include `post_topic` so override retries and trace hints work for the new tool. Legacy `post_simple_topic` media/raw-media-ref and source/author-count guards remain in their existing order before the new split block-required guard. Syntax parse passed.

Rewrote `TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT`: role is now `BNDC live-update writer`; `post_topic` is named as the primary publishing tool; legacy `post_simple_topic` and `post_sectioned_topic` are listed once as deprecated/backward-compatible aliases; the media-to-sectioned rule was removed; editorial minimalism now states a single creator dropping one artifact is exactly one `intro` block with media attached, and warns against splitting one video into `The Video` / `Audio` / `Community Reaction` sections; brevity guidance now specifies intro body 1-3 sentences / roughly 30-150 words, section body 1-2 sentences, no bullet lists in posted prose, no padding, and no filler restatement of the title; a worked NebSH-style one-video example shows exactly one intro block with a `media_ref`, followed by a labeled `Don't do this` counter-example using three sections. Preserved the per-block source attribution rule, per-block media attachment rule, canonical media-ref shape, no-global-Sources-footer rule, block type guidance, and message_id-not-CDN guidance. Grep found no tests asserting the retired prompt strings (`BNDC live-update topic editor`, media-to-sectioned wording, or `post_sectioned_topic over`).
